heading: Study and report on voters who register by mail and use of Social Security information
label: 20984 Study and report on voters who register by mail and use of Social Security information
notes: References in TextFor the effective dates of subsecs. (a)(5) and (b)(2) of , referred to in subsecs. (a)(2) and (b), see .section 21083 of this titlesection 21083(d) of this title
CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 15384 of Title 42

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission shall conduct a study of the impact of on voters who register by mail.section 21083(b) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: an examination of the impact of on first time mail registrant voters who vote in person, including the impact of such section on voter registration;section 21083(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: an examination of the impact of such section on the accuracy of voter rolls, including preventing ineligible names from being placed on voter rolls and ensuring that all eligible names are placed on voter rolls; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an analysis of the impact of such section on existing State practices, such as the use of signature verification or attestation procedures to verify the identity of voters in elections for Federal office, and an analysis of other changes that may be made to improve the voter registration process, such as verification or additional information on the registration card.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 18 months after the date on which takes effect, the Commission shall submit a report to the President and Congress on the study conducted under paragraph (1)(A) together with such recommendations for administrative and legislative action as the Commission determines is appropriate.section 21083(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Use of Social Security information
children.label: b Use of Social Security information
children.text: Not later than 18 months after the date on which takes effect, the Commission, in consultation with the Commissioner of Social Security, shall study and report to Congress on the feasibility and advisability of using Social Security identification numbers or other information compiled by the Social Security Administration to establish voter registration or other election law eligibility or identification requirements, including the matching of relevant information specific to an individual voter, the impact of such use on national security issues, and whether adequate safeguards or waiver procedures exist to protect the privacy of an individual voter.section 21083(a)(5) of this title
